title: Xtra Crumbly Japanese Ground Beef Bowl
description: A garlicky and crumbly Japanese dish featuring ground ginger and namul-blanch-seasoned vegetables, served in a lacquerware bowl.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium-high heat.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the ground ginger and garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and 2 tablespoons of water, then pour the mixture over the beef and stir to combine.
Add the namul vegetables to the skillet and cook until they are tender, about 3-4 minutes.
Season the mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
To assemble the bowls, divide the cooked white rice between four lacquerware bowls.
Top the rice with the beef and vegetable mixture, then sprinkle with green onions.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
